meaning: n.蜂群,一大群v.涌往,挤满,密集,成群浮游,云集
examples: A swarm of people encircled the hotel.
At the time, this was the largest swarm of robots ever tested.
A network swarm is all edges and therefore open-ended any way you come at it.
It is easier to retreat and compose yourself after every score than swarm about, arms flailing.
All the tech writers in attendance rush past them, and instead swarm around... a keyboard.
